mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities and yields positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a win rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, reducing recession probability and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, prompting rapid equity sell-offs and negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below the threshold.
